Solve \(x^2 + 5x - 6 \leq 0\).
A. \(-6 \leq x \leq 1\)
B. \(-3 \leq x \leq -2\)
C. \(-1 \leq x \leq 6\)
D. \(x \leq -6\) or \(x \geq 1\)
E. \(x \leq -1\) or \(x \geq 6\)

Solve the simultaneous equations: \(\begin{cases} 2x + 3y = -1 \\ x - y = 7 \end{cases}\)
A. \((0, -3)\)
B. \((1, -1)\)
C. \((4, -3)\)
D. \((4, -3)\)
E. \((2, -1)\)

If the equation \(x^2 - 6x + k = 0\) has real roots, find all possible values of \(k\).
A. \(k \geq 9\)
B. \(k \geq -9\)
C. \(k = 9\)
D. \(k \leq 9\)
E. \(k \leq -9\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a square of side \(10\text{ cm}\). If \(AE = AF\) and the area of \(\Delta CEF\) is \(20\text{ cm}^2\), which of the following equations can be used to find \(AF\)?
A. \(x^2 + 10(10-x) + 20 = 100\)
B. \(x^2 + 20(10-x) + 20 = 100\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2}x^2 + 10x + 20 = 100\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2}x^2 + 20(10-x) = 100\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 10(10-x) \cdot 2 + 20 = 100\)

The figure shows the graph of \(y = ax^2 + bx + c\). Which of the following is true?
A. \(a > 0, c > 0\) and \(b^2 - 4ac > 0\)
B. \(a > 0, c > 0\) and \(b^2 - 4ac < 0\)
C. \(a > 0, c < 0\) and \(b^2 - 4ac < 0\)
D. \(a < 0, c > 0\) and \(b^2 - 4ac > 0\)
E. \(a < 0, c < 0\) and \(b^2 - 4ac > 0\)

If \(a, b, c, d\) are consecutive terms of an arithmetic sequence, which of the following must be true?
I. \(b - a = d - c\)
II. \(d, c, b, a\) are consecutive terms of an arithmetic sequence
III. \(a < b < c < d\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

A man bought a box of \(200\) apples for \(\$500\). \(10\) of the apples were rotten and the rest were sold at \(\$4\) each. Find his percentage profit correct to \(2\) significant figures.
A. \(34\%\)
B. \(38\%\)
C. \(52\%\)
D. \(57\%\)
E. \(60\%\)

If \(a < b\) and \(c < d\), which of the following must be true?
I. \(ac < bd\)
II. \(a+b < c+d\)
III. \(a-b < c-d\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

If \(x\) varies inversely as \(y\) and directly as \(z^2\), then
A. \(xz^2\) is a constant.
B. \(\dfrac{x}{z^2}\) is a constant.
C. \(xz^2\) is a constant.
D. \(\dfrac{z^2}{y}\) is a constant.
E. \(\dfrac{1}{x} + \dfrac{z^2}{y}\) is a constant.

In the figure, \(OAB\) is an equilateral triangle. Find the bearing of \(B\) from \(A\).
A. \(10^\circ\)
B. \(80^\circ\)
C. \(170^\circ\)
D. \(260^\circ\)
E. \(350^\circ\)

In the figure, the radii of the two circles are \(3\text{ cm}\) and \(1\text{ cm}\) respectively. Find the ratio of the area of the shaded part to that of the smaller circle.
A. \(2:1\)
B. \(3:1\)
C. \(4:1\)
D. \(8:1\)
E. \(9:1\)

The figure shows a right circular cone of base radius \(4\text{ cm}\) and height \(3\text{ cm}\). Find the area of its curved surface.
A. \(12\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(16\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(20\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(24\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(48\pi\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, find the area of the pentagon \(ABCDE\).
A. \(16\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(18\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \(20\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \(24\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \(32\text{ cm}^2\)

The figure shows a test tube consisting of a cylindrical upper part of radius \(1\text{ cm}\) and a hemispherical lower part of the same radius. If the height of the test tube is \(12\text{ cm}\), find its capacity.
A. \(\dfrac{33}{3}\pi\text{ cm}^3\)
B. \(\dfrac{37}{3}\pi\text{ cm}^3\)
C. \(\dfrac{38}{3}\pi\text{ cm}^3\)
D. \(\dfrac{40}{3}\pi\text{ cm}^3\)
E. \(\dfrac{68}{3}\pi\text{ cm}^3\)

In the figure, \(OABC\) is a sector. Find the area of the shaded region.
A. \((\pi - 2)\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \((2\pi - 4)\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \((4\pi - 8)\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \((8\pi - 8)\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \((8\pi - 16)\text{ cm}^2\)

In the figure, find \(CD\).
A. \(6\text{ cm}\)
B. \(4\text{ cm}\)
C. \(4\sqrt{3}\text{ cm}\)
D. \(2\sqrt{3}\text{ cm}\)
E. \(3\text{ cm}\)

In the figure, find \(x\) correct to 3 significant figures.
A. \(48.2\)
B. \(55.1\)
C. \(58.4\)
D. \(67.5\)
E. \(73.4\)

In the figure, \(PQRS\) is a trapezium. Find \(x\) correct to 3 significant figures.
A. \(3.01\)
B. \(5.57\)
C. \(5.77\)
D. \(6.00\)
E. \(9.54\)

In the figure, \(PQ\) and \(RS\) are two vertical poles standing on the horizontal ground. The angle of elevation of \(R\) from \(P\) is \(20^\circ\) and the angle of depression of \(S\) from \(P\) is \(40^\circ\). If \(RS = 5\text{ m}\), then \(PR =\)
A. \(\dfrac{5\sin 40^\circ}{\sin 70^\circ}\text{ m}\)
B. \(\dfrac{5\sin 50^\circ}{\sin 60^\circ}\text{ m}\)
C. \(\dfrac{5\sin 60^\circ}{\sin 50^\circ}\text{ m}\)
D. \(\dfrac{5\sin 70^\circ}{\sin 40^\circ}\text{ m}\)
E. \(\dfrac{5\sin 50^\circ}{\sin 60^\circ}\text{ m}\)

In the figure, \(AB\) is a diameter of the circle and \(ABD\) is a straight line. \(\angle CBD =\)
A. \(2\theta\)
B. \(4\theta\)
C. \(90^\circ + \theta\)
D. \(180^\circ - \theta\)
E. \(180^\circ - 2\theta\)

In the figure, \(AD\) is a diameter of the circle. If \(AB : BC : CD = 3 : 5 : 7\), then \(\angle ADC =\)
A. \(36^\circ\)
B. \(45^\circ\)
C. \(48^\circ\)
D. \(49^\circ\)
E. \(72^\circ\)

In the figure, \(AB = BC = CA = CD\). Find \(\angle CBD\).
A. \(20^\circ\)
B. \(25^\circ\)
C. \(27.5^\circ\)
D. \(30^\circ\)
E. \(35^\circ\)

In the figure, \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} BC\). Find \(B\) correct to 3 significant figures.
A. \(0.775\text{ cm}\)
B. \(1.00\text{ cm}\)
C. \(1.34\text{ cm}\)
D. \(1.73\text{ cm}\)
E. \(1.80\text{ cm}\)

Find the equation of the line passing through \((3, -1)\) and parallel to \(5x + 4y = 0\).
A. \(4x - 5y + 9 = 0\)
B. \(4x + 5y + 1 = 0\)
C. \(5x - 4y + 9 = 0\)
D. \(5x + 4y - 1 = 0\)
E. \(5x + 4y + 1 = 0\)

In the figure, \(PQRS\) is a parallelogram. Find the slope of \(PR\).
A. \(1/3\)
B. \(1/5\)
C. \(9/11\)
D. \(11/9\)
E. \(-5/9\)

In the figure, \(C_A\) and \(C_B\) are the cumulative frequency curves of two distributions of weights \(A\) and \(B\) respectively. Which of the following is/are true?
I. median of \(A >\) median of \(B\)
II. range of \(A >\) range of \(B\)
III. inter-quartile range of \(A >\) inter-quartile range of \(B\)
A. I only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

Two cards are drawn randomly from five cards numbered \(2, 2, 3, 5\) and \(5\) respectively. Find the probability that the sum of the numbers on the cards drawn is \(5\).
A. \(1/5\)
B. \(2/5\)
C. \(1/10\)
D. \(2/25\)
E. \(4/25\)

In a shooting game, the probability that Mr. Tung will hit the target is \(2/3\). If he shoots twice, find the probability that he will hit the target at least once.
A. \(1/9\)
B. \(2/9\)
C. \(4/9\)
D. \(2/3\)
E. \(8/9\)

Let \(a\) and \(b\) be two consecutive positive integers. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(a+b\) is odd.
II. \(ab\) is odd.
III. \(a^2 + b^2\) is odd.
A. III only
B. I and II only
C. I and III only
D. II and III only
E. I, II and III

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a trapezium. Which of the following must be true?
I. \(\Delta AED\) is an equilateral triangle
II. \(EBCD\) is a parallelogram
III. \(AB = 2DC\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. I and II only
D. I and III only
E. I, II and III

If \(b < 0\) and \(c < 0\), which of the following shaded regions may represent the solution of \(x + by + c \geq 0\)?
A. A
B. B
C. C
D. D
E. E

The figure shows a right pyramid with a square base is divided into three parts \(A\), \(B\) and \(C\) by two planes parallel to the base such that the lengths of their slant edges are \(1\text{ cm}\), \(2\text{ cm}\) and \(3\text{ cm}\) respectively. Find volume of \(A\) : volume of \(B\) : volume of \(C\).
A. \(1:2:3\)
B. \(1:4:9\)
C. \(1:8:27\)
D. \(1:7:19\)
E. \(1:27:216\)

Find the sum to infinity of geometric series \(\dfrac{1}{x} + \dfrac{1}{x^2} + \dots\), where \(x > 1\).
A. \(\dfrac{1}{x-1}\)
B. \(\dfrac{1}{x+1}\)
C. \(\dfrac{x}{x-1}\)
D. \(\dfrac{x}{x+1}\)
E. \(\dfrac{x}{x+1}\)

\(\dfrac{1+\sin\theta}{\cos\theta} + \dfrac{\cos\theta}{1+\sin\theta} =\)
A. \(1\)
B. \(2(1+\sin\theta)\)
C. \(2\cos\theta\)
D. \(\dfrac{2}{\cos\theta}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1+\sin\theta+\cos\theta}{\cos\theta(1+\sin\theta)}\)

The figure shows the graph of the function
A. \(y = \cos x\).
B. \(y = \cos(-x)\).
C. \(y = \cos(\pi-x)\).
D. \(y = \cos(\pi/2+x)\).
E. \(y = \cos(\pi-x)\).

In the figure, \(ABC\) is a semicircle. Find the area of the shaded part.
A. \(6\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
B. \(15\pi\text{ cm}^2\)
C. \((6\pi - 9\sqrt{3})\text{ cm}^2\)
D. \((6\pi + 9\sqrt{3})\text{ cm}^2\)
E. \((12\pi - 9\sqrt{3})\text{ cm}^2\)

For \(0^\circ \leq x \leq 360^\circ\), how many roots does the equation \(3\sin^2 x + 2\sin x - 1 = 0\) have?
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(2\)
D. \(3\)
E. \(4\)

The figure shows a right pyramid with a square base \(ABCD\). Let \(\theta\) be the angle between the planes \(VAB\) and \(VCD\). Find \(\sin \theta\).
A. \(\sqrt{2}/2\)
B. \(\sqrt{2}/3\)
C. \(\sqrt{3}/3\)
D. \(\sqrt{3}/2\)
E. \(\sqrt{6}/3\)

In the figure, \(CE\) is tangent to the circle at \(C\). Find \(\angle DCE\).
A. \(40^\circ\)
B. \(42^\circ\)
C. \(49^\circ\)
D. \(54^\circ\)
E. \(78^\circ\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\), \(AFE\), \(CGE\) and \(FGD\) are straight lines. If \(AB = BC = 2CD\), then \(CG : GE =\)
A. \(1:2\)
B. \(1:3\)
C. \(1:4\)
D. \(1:5\)
E. \(1:6\)

Find the mean deviation of the five numbers \(x-2, x-1, x, x+1\) and \(x+2\).
A. \(x\)
B. \(0\)
C. \(6/5\)
D. \(\sqrt{2}\)
E. \(5\)

The circle \(x^2 + y^2 - 2x - 7y - 8 = 0\) intersects the x-axis at \(A\) and \(B\). Find the length of \(AB\).
A. \(2\)
B. \(6\)
C. \(7\)
D. \(9\)
E. \(\sqrt{85}\)

The equations of two circles are \(x^2 + y^2 + ax - by = 0\) and \(x^2 + y^2 - ax + by = 0\). Which of the following must be true?
I. The two circles have the same centre.
II. The two circles have equal radii.
III. The line joining the centres of the two circles passes through origin.
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

\(A(7, 14)\) and \(B(1, 2)\) are two points. \(C\) is a point on \(AB\) produced such that \(AB : BC = 2 : 1\). Find the coordinates of \(C\).
A. \((-5, -10)\)
B. \((-2, -4)\)
C. \((3, 6)\)
D. \((5, 10)\)
E. \((10, 20)\)
